site.name: Open Health Data Portal
site.publisher: Ministry of Innovation and Digital Transformation

home.title: Bulgaria's health data — open and verifiable
home.lead: MEDATA brings healthcare spending and activity together in one place and makes it machine-readable, freely accessible and interoperable. Published as DCAT-AP, with independent disclosure control.
home.exploreLead: Current datasets, published as DCAT-AP and available programmatically via a stable API.
home.chartsLead: Sample aggregates from the pilot datasets. Full data, breakdowns and sources are on each dataset's page.
home.chartsMoney: Reimbursed amount by RHIF (hospital drugs outside pathways, 2025)
home.chartsCases: Number of cases by RHIF (clinical pathway activity, 2025)
mission: The initiative continues the spirit of the civic project "Diagnosis Bulgaria" — a public effort to gather scattered health data in one place and make it understandable to everyone.

The goal is simple: public health data should be open, machine-readable and verifiable — for citizens, journalists, researchers and institutions. Transparency is a precondition for trust and for better decisions in the system.

pipeline.title: Collection
pipeline.body: Data is received from the holders (Ministry of Health, NHIF, etc.) as files or via API — already anonymized and aggregated.

pipeline.title: Disclosure control
pipeline.body: Small cells are suppressed independently, to prevent re-identification of patients when data is combined.

pipeline.title: Catalog
pipeline.body: Each dataset is described in DCAT-AP and published in a catalog compatible for harvesting with data.egov.bg and data.europa.eu.

pipeline.title: API and downloads
pipeline.body: Data is available as CSV, JSON, XLSX and RDF through a versioned, public read API.

principles: Public repository from day 1 — transparency by default.
API-first: every dataset is available programmatically via a stable versioned API.
Disclosure control: small cells are suppressed to prevent re-identification.

datasets.title: Datasets
datasets.lead: All published datasets. Select one for details, metadata and downloads.

docs.title: Technical documentation
docs.lead: How to use the API, the metadata profile and harvesting formats. Everything is public, versioned and needs no registration.
docs.apiLead: API-first (art. 41): every dataset is available programmatically at a stable address, with a /v1 version and at least 24 months of support.
docs.catalogBody: The catalog core is CKAN. Metadata is published as DCAT-AP 3.0.1 and is available for automatic harvesting by data.egov.bg and data.europa.eu.
formats: CSV — for tabular tools and spreadsheets.
JSON — for web apps and scripts.
XLSX — for working directly in Excel.
RDF / JSON-LD — for semantic harvesting via DCAT-AP.

about.title: About the portal
about.lead: A joint inter-institutional open health data initiative, institutionally hosted by the Ministry of Innovation and Digital Transformation.
about.originTitle: From civic audit to public infrastructure
origin: MEDATA continues the civic project "Diagnosis Bulgaria" — a platform that gathered and processed millions of rows of official information about the country's hospitals and showed where the money for health goes.

What began as an independent civic audit becomes here a durable, institutional and open infrastructure: the same transparency, but with a legal framework, longevity and interoperability with the national and European open-data ecosystem.
governance: Institutional host: MIDT — provides the framework and longevity, without being the sole technical implementer.
Data holders: the Ministry of Health, NHIF and others — supply already anonymized and aggregated data.
The portal adds independent disclosure control and publishes to open standards.
Development is open and community-driven — public repository, public processes, public rules.
standards: API-first with versioning and content negotiation (CSV/JSON/XLSX/RDF).
Open license and public source code from day 1.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: Questions, suggestions, data reports and proposals for new datasets.
contact.repoBody: The project is open. The code, the rules and the decision history are public — suggestions and contributions are welcome.
contact.security: Security vulnerabilities are disclosed responsibly — see SECURITY.md, not as a public issue.

footer.tagline: Open, machine-readable and verifiable health data for Bulgaria.
footer.license: Content is available under open terms. The code is public.
